creator: andrews@TECHNOLOGIST.COM
text:
This report was sent to me by Trevor Johnson <trevor@jpj.net>:

joe creates a file, DEADJOE, when it is killed. Unfortunately, this file
is by default world readable. When somebody is editing sensitive files,
this program can reveal the contents, thus causing a security breach.

How-To-Repeat: 
Use joe to edit a file, then kill -HUP its process. The resulting DEADJOE
will be world readable (and writable).
